description_long: This police station is a single-story building with a gabled, reddish-brown roof and a combination of stone and white plaster exterior walls. The main entrance is marked by a prominent archway and is flanked by blue-framed windows and doors, giving the facade a welcoming yet official appearance. The structure includes a secondary, smaller wing attached to the main building, likely serving as an annex or garage. The building sits on a rectangular gray base, suggesting a paved lot or foundation. The stonework on the facade adds a sense of durability and security, while the blue accents provide a distinctive, civic look. The overall design is functional and modest, suitable for a neighborhood police station in an urban setting.
description: A modest, single-story police station with a gabled red-brown roof, stone and white walls, and blue-framed windows and doors. The building features a main entrance with an archway and a secondary annex structure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with an archway, stone facade, blue doors and windows, and the attached annex on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gabled roof, white plaster walls, and the rear of the annex structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the main building and annex, with visible stone and white wall sections and blue window frames.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the gabled rooflines of both the main building and annex, with blue windows and a secondary entrance.
attribute_building_facade_features: Stone and plaster walls, blue-framed windows and doors, main entrance with archway, gabled roof, secondary annex structure.
